$comment: Branch-protection ruleset for `main`. Source-of-truth for AC-11 of slice 050. Apply via `gh api -X PUT repos/mgoodric/security-atlas/branches/main/protection --input .github/branch-protection.json`. Keep this file in sync with reality — if the maintainer adjusts via the UI or `gh api`, regenerate via `gh api repos/mgoodric/security-atlas/branches/main/protection > .github/branch-protection.json` and re-commit.

$deviations_from_slice_050_AC11.required_approving_review_count: Slice 050 AC-11 originally required ≥1 approving review. Dropped to 0 (required_pull_request_reviews: null) post-batch-13 because the maintainer is solo — no second reviewer exists. The audit trail still rides on PR + CI + squash-merge for every change. Re-evaluate when the contributor base grows past ~3 active committers. Tracked in docs/RELEASE_READINESS.md §10.
$deviations_from_slice_050_AC11.restrictions: Slice 050 AC-11 specified push restrictions to mgoodric + release-please. GitHub only supports restrictions on organization repos; this is a personal user repo, so the field is null. If the project migrates to an org account later, re-add the block.
$deviations_from_slice_050_AC11.required_status_checks.contexts: Slice 050 AC-11 enumerated 10 checks including some speculative names (Frontend · build + lint + test, Python · lint, Lint · pre-commit, codecov/project, DCO). Updated to actual workflow names. codecov/project and DCO removed pending the maintainer wiring those services — tracked in docs/RELEASE_READINESS.md §10. Analyze (python) removed because the repo currently has zero Python source (oscal-bridge hasn't shipped yet); re-add when the slice that lands oscal-bridge merges.

$additions_from_slice_128.actions_pin_check_required: Slice 128 added `actions-pin-check` to required-checks. It is the BLOCKING (not informational) supply-chain mitigation that asserts every `uses:` line in every workflow under `.github/workflows/*.yml` is pinned to a 40-character commit SHA (not a floating tag). Unlike slice 127 / 069 / 089 / 109 / 120's informational-job convention, this check fails the build on violation — the discipline must hold continuously or the tag-jacking supply-chain attack surface re-opens. After merge: run `bash scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh` to push the file's contexts list to live GitHub branch-protection on `main`. See `docs/audit-log/128-sha-pin-all-github-actions-decisions.md` D3 for the coordination with slice 127.

$additions_from_slice_140.openapi_drift_check_required: Slice 140 added `openapi-drift-check` to required-checks. It is the BLOCKING (not informational) spec-discipline guard that asserts `docs/openapi.yaml` is in lockstep with (1) the `cmd/atlas-openapi` generator output AND (2) the chi route registrations under `internal/api/*/`. Same precedent as slice 128's `actions-pin-check`: discipline that must hold continuously gets a BLOCKING guard, not an informational one — a misleading spec is silent control degradation (operators code against a stale contract). After merge: run `bash scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh` to push the file's contexts list to live GitHub branch-protection on `main`. See `docs/audit-log/140-openapi-spec-decisions.md` D3.

$slice_158_drift_detector_redesign: Slice 158 (PR #311 follow-on) redesigned the drift-detector CI surface introduced by slice 127. PR #311's wrong fix added `administration: read` to the GITHUB_TOKEN permissions block; `administration` is not a valid scope, actionlint flags it, GHA silently rejected the workflow file at parse, and slice 127's drift detector never actually ran. Slice 158 fixes the root cause: the PR-time job (`branch-protection-drift-validate`) validates only the file shape (no `gh api` call, no elevated token), and a separate push-on-main job (`branch-protection-drift-live`) does the live compare using `secrets.BRANCH_PROTECTION_READ_TOKEN` (fine-grained PAT with `Administration: Read` scope, env-scoped to the one step that needs it). Slice 158 also adds an actionlint pre-commit + CI guard so the same invalid-scope mistake cannot recur. The required-checks contexts list is UNCHANGED by this slice — both new jobs remain informational, matching the slice 127 P0-A1 posture. See `docs/adr/0005-branch-protection-pat-vs-app.md` for the PAT-vs-App D1 decision + maintainer setup steps.

$deviations_from_slice_069.frontend_vitest_and_playwright_not_requi: Slice 069 added `Frontend · vitest` and `Frontend · Playwright e2e` to this file as required-checks, but the contexts were never applied to the live GitHub branch-protection config — silent drift (file moved forward; the `gh api PUT` never ran). The drift caused real harm during the 2026-05-17/18 cascade-unblock session: four PRs (#234, #259, #262, #264) sat held for hours on a phantom-blocker rationale. Slice 127 reconciles via option (a) (file-to-live) — the file is the side that moved, but the practical constraint is that 4 e2e specs are still broken (slice 123 territory: auth-open-redirect, first-time-login, logo-render, security-headers) and applying option (b) (file-to-restore) right now would block every PR — including slice 127's own PR — on those known-failing specs. The restoration to option (b) is filed as a follow-up: once slice 123 lands and unblocks the 4 specs, re-add `Frontend · vitest` + `Frontend · Playwright e2e` here and run `bash scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh`. Slice 127 also ships `scripts/check-branch-protection.sh` + a `branch-protection-drift` informational CI job so the next drift event surfaces as a sticky PR comment instead of waiting hours of debugging.

$rationale_required_signatures_off: Signed-commit enforcement adds friction for community drive-by contributors (commits via the GitHub UI editor are signed by the GitHub bot, but commits from clones need a configured signing key). DCO sign-off + Conventional-Commit lint give us provenance and changelog discipline without raising the contributor bar. Revisit if the contributor base grows past ~25 active committers and trust-signal demand changes.

$verification.1: A test PR with a failing CI check must NOT be mergeable. Mergeability resolves only after all checks in required_status_checks.contexts return green.
$verification.2: A direct `git push origin main` from a clone (bypassing PR) MUST be rejected by the server.
$verification.3: A `git push --force origin main` MUST be rejected even for the maintainer (enforce_admins=true).
